results.notes: Core REQ mappings found in daemon/scorer/MCP/shim code, but path spelling drift prevents clean pass.
Completed CHK items cite docs without file-line anchors.
Integrity/rebuild helper exists but is not wired into live DB open/query paths; concurrent rebuild lacks a guard.
Daemon shutdown publishes unavailable, closes watcher, releases lease; plugin subprocess timeout resolves through close/error after SIGTERM/SIGKILL.
No prompt-text leak found; filesystem/process path diagnostics are exposed.
Python fallback parses JSON/SQLite without executing graph metadata; probe uses status freshness/PID evidence rather than stale lock file alone.
Bridge import path is static and cache key hashes prompt/options/source signature/workspace.
Stale direct MCP output carries warnings and model-visible brief says Advisor: stale.
Public skill_graph_scan can reindex and publish generation without trusted-caller gating.
REQ-001 through REQ-007 mostly map to implementation; REQ-008 promotion gates map only to advisor_validate slices, not the claimed gate-bundle artifact; REQ-010 remains open.
16 of 16 completed CHK items lack file:line citations; examples enumerated in iteration markdown.
Advisor catalog exists, but derived_generated weight is documented as 0.10 while runtime weights-config uses 0.15.
Corrupt DB recovery, daemon restart, and unavailable/absent modes have playbook coverage; external public scan caller authority is missing.
Six root-doc runtime package references use mcp_server/skill-advisor while runtime uses mcp_server/skill_advisor.
Only ADR-001 exists; public scan trust model and separate promotion-gate contract are implicit.
Python/plugin/TS references are present but mix stale legacy paths with current mcp_server/skill_advisor and plugin bridge locations.
All four requested active issue invariants lack targeted regression tests, producing DR-008-D3-P1-001.
Main reindex path uses withBusyRetry; quarantine insert/recover/count bypass the same helper.
Lane modules are clear, but lane addition requires synchronized edits across types, empty score shape, fusion, weights, schema, and docs.
README documents the intent; executable contract shapes are duplicated across TS daemon probe, Python inline bridge, OpenCode bridge, and plugin parser.
Advisor handlers use Zod output schemas; skill-graph handlers hand-roll local HandlerResponse/okResponse/errorResponse helpers.
README identifies plugin plus bridge boundary and stable compat entrypoint; bridge imports dist/skill_advisor/compat/index.js.
SQLite skill-graph fixture setup is duplicated between advisor DB tests and server skill-graph handler tests.
Seven shipped sub-tracks are collapsed into a single umbrella ADR.
No orphaned import proven; path drift remains prior finding and source-signature hyphen path is not re-flagged.
